chunk_summary: This section discusses phenotype-driven nutrition decisions in GI disorders, using clinical vignettes to guide safe feeding practices, diagnostic integrity, and parallel management of nutrition and disease etiology.
learning_objectives: Understand phenotype-driven nutrition strategies for GI disorders like persistent diarrhea, celiac disease, and Crohn's disease.
Learn to balance safe feeding practices with diagnostic integrity in malabsorption syndromes.
Recognize the importance of monitoring and stepwise advancement in refeeding syndrome management.
key_facts: Case 1: For persistent diarrhea, use IAP Diet A (low-lactose, energy-dense) with ORS and zinc; reassess in 7 days.
Case 2: Resume adequate gluten intake before repeating tTG-IgA and total IgA tests for celiac diagnosis; confirm with biopsy.
Case 3: In suspected Crohn’s disease with minimal intake, start EEN, check baseline phosphate, potassium, magnesium, and prescribe thiamine.
Refeeding syndrome requires stepwise calorie advancement and monitoring of electrolytes to prevent complications.
Across cases, avoid unnecessary dietary eliminations and ensure time-stamped diagnostic stop rules.
reference_quote: IAP Standard Treatment Guidelines recommend IAP Diet A for persistent diarrhea with energy density preserved and low lactose intake.
document_key_topics: Safe feeding practices in malabsorption syndromes
Diagnostic integrity in celiac disease and Crohn’s disease
